fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Datadog shows strong revenue and earnings growth with healthy cash flow, but operates with high valuation multiples and thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $936.73M TTM demonstrates robust cash generation.
High gross margins of 79.9% suggest pricing power and efficient service delivery.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target price near the current spot price, indicating positive external sentiment.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 559.13 and Forward P/E of 78.62 represent very high valuation multiples.
Operating margin of 0.8% and Profit margin of 3.7% are thin, suggesting potential vulnerability despite revenue growth.
Return on assets is negative (-0.2%), indicating inefficient use of assets.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 is high, though current and quick ratios suggest good short-term liquidity.

news.paragraph: Datadog recently reported its first quarter with over $1 billion in revenue and raised its 2026 outlook, signaling strong performance and positive investor sentiment, as evidenced by a prominent analyst adding the stock to his AI ETF. The company's ability to achieve significant revenue growth and positive earnings surprises, coupled with a high consensus 'strong buy' rating from analysts, suggests continued momentum. Investors will be watching the upcoming August earnings report for further confirmation of this upward trajectory.
